🚀 We’re hiring at Avery Law!

We’re looking for a corporate paralegal to join our growing team at Avery Law, with the genuine intention of offering a training contract following a successful period with the firm.

You’ll work closely with partners, associates, trainees and consultant solicitors in a fast‑moving, collaborative environment, getting hands‑on experience on corporate and venture transactions. Proper exposure. Proper responsibility.

This hire also reflects how we think about the future of the profession.

There’s a lot of hype about AI replacing junior lawyers. Our view is different: AI changes how lawyers are trained; it doesn’t remove the need to train them properly.

We want to develop the next generation of corporate lawyers who understand judgment, deal dynamics, founders and investors, and who know how to use AI intelligently, not hide behind it.

👉 If you know someone sharp who’d thrive in this environment, please share this.

🔗 View the role and apply here: https://lnkd.in/eBgMjXjV

— The Cap Lawyer

The AI job market isn’t slowing down — it’s evolving faster than most people realize.
What I’ve been seeing lately is a clear shift: companies are no longer hiring generalists.
 They’re looking for people who can evaluate, refine, and guide AI systems with precision.
This means something important:
There’s a growing demand for specialists in areas like
 • Linguistics
 • Research & Analysis
 • Data Strategy
 • AI Evaluation
And these roles are not only remote — many of them are high-paying contract opportunities.
The problem?
 Most of these positions never reach traditional job boards. They stay hidden behind platforms that match talent directly, bypassing the usual “apply and wait” cycle.
I’ve been tracking one of these platforms closely, and it’s clear they’re actively onboarding people with the right skill sets right now.
Instead of keeping it to myself, I’m sharing direct referral access.
If you’re serious about entering the AI space — or upgrading to better remote opportunities — this is worth exploring.
Access current openings and get fast-tracked here:
 👉 https://t.mercor.com/BjycJ

Hiring | Freelance Lingerie Designer (Tech Pack Specialist) | Contract | Mumbai Preferred

We are looking for an experienced Lingerie Designer to join us on a contractual basis — someone who can own the design-to-production handoff independently.

The Role
You will be responsible for designing lingerie and intimate wear styles and developing complete, factory-ready tech packs from scratch.

What You Must Bring
Proven experience in lingerie and intimate wear design

Mandatory: Strong knowledge of knit fabrics — stretch percentage, recovery, and behavior on the body
Lingerie construction expertise — underwire channeling, molded cups, elastic application, hook & eye placement, strap hardware
Proficiency in Adobe Illustrator for technical flat sketches

Ability to develop end-to-end tech packs including:
— Technical flat sketches (front, back, and detail views)
— Bill of Materials (BOM) with fabric, trims, and hardware callouts
— Size spec sheets with Points of Measure (POM)
— Grading rules across size range
— Stitch, seam, and construction details
— Label and packaging specs
— Fit comment and revision tracking

Working knowledge of Excel / Google Sheets for spec and BOM documentation
Experience liaising with manufacturers and interpreting fit feedback into spec corrections


Good to Have
CLO 3D or Browzwear experience — strongly preferred, especially for virtual prototyping of stretch and knit styles
Familiarity with AI-integrated design tools (Midjourney, Adobe Firefly, or similar) for mood boarding, concept development, or print/pattern exploration
Experience with PDM/PLM tools (Backbone, Notion-based systems, or similar)

Location: Mumbai-based candidates will be given preference. Open to remote for the right profile.

Compensation: Competitive, as per industry standards and commensurate with experience.

Portfolio-based shortlisting. Please share your portfolio and relevant work samples when reaching out.

Hiring for C2C role:
Job Title: Sr. Python Developer w/d (Athena or Sec DB or Quartz) Exp 
Location: Jersey City, NJ or Houton, TX – 5 Days onsite role. (Locals Only)
Long Term Contract
 
Role Description: Market Risk Technology is responsible for providing Risk Managers with the capability to assess the financial impact of various adverse market scenarios at a firm level. This capability covers calculations, scenario analysis, analytics, reporting and other tools and processes required to manager risk. Our team develop the firm’s strategic calculation (e.g. VaR, Stress), Time Series, and Scenario Management Platforms for Firmwide Market Risk which Integrate with our front office risk management systems,
As a Software Engineer, you serve as a seasoned member of an agile team to design and deliver trusted market leading technology products in a secure, stable, and scalable way. You are responsible for carrying out critical technology solutions across multiple technical areas within various business functions in support of the firm’s business objectives
Job responsibilities:
• Interface closely with the business, quantitative research analysts, and upstream as well as downstream technology teams across the firm
• Work in Agile environment, always looking to Improve
• Executes software solutions, design, development, and technical troubleshooting with ability to think beyond routine or conventional approaches to build solutions or break down technical problems
• Contributes to software engineering communities of practice and events that explore new and emerging technologies
• Proactively identifies hidden problems and patterns in data and uses these insights to drive improvements to coding hygiene and system architecture
• Produces architecture and design artifacts for complex applications while being accountable for ensuring design constraints are met by software code development
• Adds to team culture of diversity, equity, inclusion, and respect
Required Skills:
Professional development experience with Python
Experience communicating to business facing customers and technical teams
Overall knowledge of the software development life cycle
Solid understanding of agile methodologies such as CI/CD, Application Resiliency, and security 
Formal training or certification on software engineering concepts and 5+ years applied experience
Handa on practical experience in system design, application development, testing, and operational stability.
Demonstrated knowledge of software applications and technical processes within a technical discipline (e.g. Cloud, AI, ML, Mobile etc)
Exposure to Banking/Finance Market Risk Domains
Experience with Athena eco system familiarity or similar (Sec DB or Quartz)
Look for Hydra DB …Athena uses in-house hydra DB

Please reach me at Arpit.yadav@diverselynx.com

Innovative Defense Technologies Transforming U.S. Warfare:

Recent developments show how firms like Anduril, Palantir, and SpaceX are reshaping America's defense strategies. These companies are introducing cutting-edge technologies that integrate artificial intelligence, data analytics, and advanced aerospace capabilities into military operations. As a result, the U.S. military is not only enhancing its operational efficiency but also adapting to new forms of warfare that rely on real-time information and rapid response.

The evolving landscape raises questions about the future of traditional defense contracts and the role of startups in government projects. As the military seeks to modernize, there may be a growing demand for agile companies capable of delivering innovative solutions quickly.

--

Making sense of what's shaping the world.
Follow for insights that actually matter.

#Hiring #W2 #C2C #DataGovernanceArchitect #DataArchitect #Austin #Local
Job Title: Data Governance & Enterprise Data Architect
Location: Austin, TX(Onsite)---Need Local to Austin, TX candidates only
 
Job Summary:
We are seeking an experienced Data Governance & Enterprise Data Architect to lead and support enterprise-wide data architecture initiatives with a strong focus on data governance, compliance, and structured data environments. This role is ideal for professionals with deep experience in traditional data architecture, large-scale data migrations, and governance frameworks.

Key Responsibilities:
•           Define and implement enterprise data architecture standards, frameworks, and governance policies
•           Lead data migration initiatives, including planning, validation, and reconciliation processes
•           Establish and manage data governance practices, including data quality, lineage, and compliance
•           Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders to align data strategies with organizational goals
•           Ensure consistency, integrity, and security of data across systems and platforms

Required Skills & Experience:
•           Strong experience in enterprise data architecture and data governance frameworks
•           Proven experience in large-scale data migrations and legacy system modernization
•           Solid understanding of data modeling, data quality, and metadata management
•           Experience working with traditional data platforms and structured data environments
•           Ability to work with cross-functional teams and drive governance initiatives

Nice to Have (Not Mandatory):
•           Exposure to cloud data platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, or BigQuery
•           Basic understanding of modern data engineering concepts (batch vs streaming)
•           Awareness of emerging trends in AI/ML and data-driven architectures

Ideal Candidate Profile:
•           Strong in governance, compliance, and structured data ecosystems
•           Comfortable operating in stable, enterprise environments rather than highly experimental setups
•           Able to provide strategic direction but may not be deeply hands-on in modern real-time or AI-driven systems

Best Regards,
Rajesh(Ken)
Cymansys Solutions LLC
📧 Email: ken@cymansys.com

India and South Korea have launched the India–Korea Digital Bridge, a long-term partnership focused on AI, semiconductors, and IT collaboration — targeting ~$50B in bilateral trade by 2030.
2️⃣ HCL Technologies — Q4 FY26 Results Incoming
HCLTech has begun its two-day board meeting (April 20–21) to review FY26 performance. Results are expected post-market on April 21, along with a potential interim dividend.
3️⃣ TCS — Salary Hikes + Record Deal Wins
TCS rolled out salary hikes across all grades effective April 1, 2026. It also reported a record TCV of $40.7B for FY26 and $12B in Q4, including 3 mega deals.
4️⃣ Wipro — ₹15,000 Cr Buyback Approved
Wipro approved a buyback of up to ₹15,000 crore at ₹250/share, subject to shareholder approval — reinforcing its capital return strategy.
5️⃣ Ola Krutrim — AI Assistant “Kruti” Offline
Kruti is currently unavailable across web and app platforms. This follows layoffs and stalled development reports, raising concerns about India’s first AI unicorn.
6️⃣ Startup India — Record Growth, Funding Slowdown
India added 55,000+ startups in FY26 (highest ever). However, funding dropped 26% YoY to $2.3B in Q1 2026, with zero $100M+ deals — signaling a tighter funding environment.
7️⃣ Flipkart — Food Delivery Entry
Flipkart is piloting food delivery in Bengaluru, with a potential full-scale launch by late 2026 — challenging the existing duopoly in a ~$24B market.
8️⃣ Oracle India — Major Layoffs
Oracle reportedly cut ~12,000 roles in India, citing AI-driven efficiencies — one of the largest layoffs by a single company this year.
9️⃣ GCC Boom — Hiring Surge Ahead
India’s Global Capability Centers (GCCs) are expected to add 120K–140K jobs in 2026, with revenues projected to cross $100B by 2030.
🔟 H1B Pressure on Indian IT
Tightening US visa policies continue to impact Indian IT firms, potentially affecting billions in contracts due to reduced onsite flexibility.

→ AI is reshaping India’s IT landscape — driving both layoffs and new opportunities simultaneously
→ India is doubling down as a global GCC hub — long-term demand remains strong despite short-term disruptions
→ Startup ecosystem is expanding, but capital is cautious — growth is broadening, but late-stage funding is tightening
📊 Bottom Line:
India Tech is at an inflection point — where AI, global partnerships, and structural shifts are redefining the future of jobs, startups, and enterprise growth.

Seems like there are hiring managers and recruiters who judge applicants unfavorably for using AI to create their resumes, but I think it's all a matter of how it's applied. 

Good Use of AI for Resumes:
1. Summarizing your experience and skills with well written clear copy. 
2. Analyzing job descriptions to create a tailored resume that highlight your specific skills and experience for that job.

Bad Use of AI for Resumes: 
1. Sprinkling in buzz words and jargon that may be accurate but don't give the reader any insight.
2. Copy and pasting AI written copy into your resume without reviewing it first.
3. Analyzing job descriptions and creating tailored resumes that are inaccurate or misleading to your actual skills and experience. 

To summarize, it doesn't matter that a candidate used AI to create their resume, as long as it's clear and accurate.

Can Retail be beyond basic automation into use of Agentic AI?

Embedding autonomous "workers" directly into cloud ecosystem. Rather than just answering questions, these agents maybe designed to plan, execute, and coordinate complex retail workflows across supply chain, storefront, and corporate offices.

1. From Generative AI to Agentic AI
While generative AI (#GenAI) focuses on creating content—like product descriptions or email copy— #AgenticAI strategy focuses on outcomes.

- The Difference: An AI agent doesn't just "chat." It would understand a goal (e.g., "Fix a stockout for high-demand sneakers"), creates a multi-step plan, select the necessary tools (inventory databases, supplier portals), and execute actions like drafting a purchase order or re-routing a shipment.
 
- The Engine: These capabilities are powered by the building AI Agent, allowing retailers to deploy pre-built agents or customize new ones to handle high-volume, repetitive tasks.
 
 2. Key Use Cases in Modern #Retail
Integrate these agents into Cloud Applications, specifically targeting the "friction points" in a retail business:

Supply Chain & Inventory
- Autonomous Resolution: Agents to monitor supply signals in real-time. If a disruption is detected, an agent can semantically analyze supplier contracts to find alternative vendors and flag compliance risks before a human manager even opens a dashboard.

Examples:

- Inventory Intelligence: Agents use Vector Search in the database to reconcile structured data (sales numbers) with unstructured data (weather reports or social trends) to predict demand spikes with higher accuracy than traditional models.
 
Front-End & Customer Experience CX 
- The Hiring Workspace: For store managers,  Agentic tools proactively rank top candidates, automate interview scheduling, and flag stalled requisitions to accelerate seasonal hiring.
 
- Sales & Marketing: Identify cross-sell opportunities by analyzing unified customer data, then autonomously launch targeted campaigns to specific segments.
 
Retail Operations & Finance
- Straight-Through Processing: In retail accounting, agents ingest invoices from multiple formats (PDF, EDI, email), perform fraud checks, match them to purchase orders, and route them for payment without manual data entry.
 
- Predictive Financial Planning: Agents can run "what-if" simulations on-demand, helping retailers understand how a 5% increase in shipping costs might impact margins.

3. The Human-in-the-Loop Security
A critical part is Governance:

- Exception Handling: Agents to handle routine work (ex: Inv. TSF) but "escalate" to human managers deviations or high-value decisions.
 
- Zero Data Sharing: While these agents run on Cloud Infrastructure, sensitive retail data—such as loyalty or proprietary pricing— must never be shared with third-party LLM providers.

Summary: Soon, focus is on the "Agentic Database" and "Agentic Apps," turning the retail tech from a record-keeping system into an active workforce.

🚨 Hot take: IT hiring in 2026 is not slowing down… it’s reshaping completely.
If you’re still thinking in terms of “job titles” and “traditional hiring cycles,” you’re already behind what’s happening in the market.
Here’s what’s actually going on right now 👇

💥 1. Skills > Titles (by a lot)
 Companies don’t care what your title is anymore.
They care if you can:
Build with AI tools
Integrate cloud systems (Azure / AWS)
Automate workflows
Deliver real outcomes fast
If you can’t show impact, the resume doesn’t matter much.

🔥 2. Mid-level talent is the most valuable (and hardest to find)
 Forget senior execs for a second.
The real war is for:
 👉 4–7 years experience professionals
Why?
Productive immediately
Not over-priced
Can operate independently
This is the most competitive hiring band in tech right now.

⚡ 3. Speed is beating process
 Top candidates are gone in days, not weeks.
Companies still running:
4–5 interview rounds
slow approvals
delayed feedback loops
…are losing talent to faster competitors.

🔁 4. Contract work is no longer “backup hiring”
 It’s becoming the default for delivery.
We’re seeing a sharp rise in:
Contract-to-hire models
Project-based teams
On-demand specialists for AI, data, cloud, and systems work
Flexibility is now a strategy, not a workaround.

🤖 5. AI is rewriting job descriptions in real time
 New roles are emerging fast:
AI Engineers
Prompt / AI Workflow Specialists
Model auditors & governance roles
DevSecOps + automation hybrids
And across all of them, one expectation stands out:
👉 “Show me how you actually use AI to work faster.”

📌 Bottom line
 IT hiring in 2026 isn’t about filling roles anymore.
It’s about:
Speed
Skills
Flexibility
Real-world execution
And the gap between companies that adapt vs. don’t is widening quickly.

Earlier today I was reading something that honestly blew my mind.

AI is now hiring humans.

There's a platform where AI agents are posting jobs for us. 

They look at applications, pick a human, tell them what to do and pay them when it's done.

The AI is the hiring manager and the human is the employee/contractor.

700,000 people signed up for this in 30 days!

Everyone keeps asking if AI is going to take their job. 

Well for people that aren't using AI, it seems AI will be using them.

There's still time to make sure you're the one in the Driver's seat and not the one that is being Driven by the AI. 

Here are a few tips that I want to share from the artrricle:

Absorb — Spend 20 mins a day watching YouTube videos on AI in your industry.

Assemble — Find 1-2 people doing cool stuff with AI and buy them lunch.

Align — Make AI your default homepage so you see it every time you open your browser.

Advance — Use it for real life. Got a big decision? Ask AI. Need to review a contract? Ask AI.

The goal right now is to disrupt yourself before someone else does it for you.

Where are you at with this? Let me know. 👇

The Data Engineer JD hasn't been updated since 2018.

And it shows.

Most JDs still read like this:

✗ "5+ years of Spark experience"
✗ "Write and maintain ETL pipelines"
✗ "Strong SQL and Python skills"

AI does all of this.
Today.
Free.

You're not hiring for these skills anymore.
You're competing with a tool that has them.

Here's what the JD should say:

===== DATA ENGINEER - 2025 to 2040 ======

You don't build pipelines. You build the system that decides which pipelines should exist. And you have the scars to prove why that distinction matters.
→ You define data contracts before a single byte moves, because fixing schema drift at 11pm is a choice, not a destiny
→ You catch what AI generates but cannot reason about - hallucinated logic, plausible-looking joins on the wrong grain, aggregations that pass code review and silently lie for months
→ You translate messy business chaos into clean, scalable models - sitting in rooms where "the data is wrong" actually means "our process is broken and nobody wants to say it"
→ You treat data quality as a product with SLAs, owners, and consequences - not a Jira ticket someone creates after the exec dashboard embarrasses the team in a board meeting
→ You make the call when there's no Stack Overflow answer - when the architecture decision is ambiguous, the deadline is real, and everyone is waiting on your judgment
→ You explain to CXOs why their number is wrong - clearly, without jargon, without blame, and somehow leave the room with more trust than you walked in with
→ You design for the team inheriting your work in 5 years - writing code and documentation for engineers who don't exist yet, in a stack that will look different, on a business that will have changed
→ You hold the context no one else holds - why this table exists, what that column actually means, which upstream source lies on the third Tuesday of every month
→ You are the last line of defense between raw data and a business decision that moves money, people, or strategy

Technical skills?
Still needed. Python. SQL. Spark. dbt. Cloud. Orchestration.

But here's the shift:
Those are now the floor.
Not the ceiling.

The engineers who survive the next 15 years:

Not the fastest pipeline builders.
The ones asking: "Should this pipeline exist?"

Not the best Spark writers.
The ones asking: "Is Spark even the right call?"

Not the ones who know every tool.
The ones who know what the data is supposed to mean.

AI owns the execution.
You own the judgment.

The job title says Data Engineer.
The job description needs to catch up.

Save this if you're hiring for a data team.
Save this if you're trying to future-proof your own career.

One question:
Is your current role preparing you for 2040 or 2018?

Repost if your team needs to see this.

The Web3 talent market (2026)

The demand is unlike anything before. Traditional financial institutions aren't tentatively exploring Web3 anymore — they're hiring aggressively.
Blockchain-related job postings grew ~45% in 2025, and 2026 is accelerating that curve.
Regulation (hello, GENIUS Act) gave institutions the green light. Now they need the talent to execute.

The scarcest — and most valuable — profiles right now: 
→ Smart contract auditors: maybe 500 qualified engineers globally. If you find one, cancel everything and interview them today. 
 → Legal & compliance experts who genuinely understand crypto-specific regulation — among the hardest hires in the entire industry. 
 → Engineers who speak both AI and blockchain fluently → Institutional BD pros who can bridge TradFi and DeFi

Why This Role Exists

Mercor partners with leading AI teams to improve the quality, usefulness, and reliability of general-purpose conversational AI systems. These systems are used across a wide range of everyday and professional scenarios, and their effectiveness depends on how clearly, accurately, and helpfully they respond to real user questions.

In coding and software engineering contexts, conversational AI systems must demonstrate correct reasoning, strong problem-solving ability, and adherence to real-world engineering best practices. This project focuses on evaluating and improving how models reason about code, generate solutions, and explain technical concepts across a variety of programming tasks and complexity levels.

✨ With the help of AI, I built a design system faster than I thought possible.

But let me be real, it wasn't magic.

I prompted, A LOT.
AI made mistakes. I caught them, fixed them, and iterated.
Rinse and repeat.

Connected Anthropic Claude Code to Figma via MCP, it read my layers, audited consistency, and flagged mismatches. I directed every decision.

Honestly, this project taught me more about design systems than anything I'd done before: tokens, nested components, state coverage, and handoff structure. I finally understood why it all matters.

That's what a real AI-native workflow looks like, not handing over the wheel, but driving faster.

"It feels like LinkedIn is swarmed with ads for AI platforms, and we’d just be seen as one of the crowd." This is the #1 concern I hear from SaaS founders. Here’s my answer:

Stop saying AI-powered.

Your buyer has heard it 500 times this week.
They've scrolled past it.
They've ignored it.
They've become immune to it.

AI-powered document processing.
AI-powered workflow automation.
AI-powered analytics platform.

It all sounds the same.
And when everything sounds the same,
nothing gets remembered.

Here's what actually works:

Name the specific workflow you replace.
Name the exact time it saves.

Not: AI-powered document processing.
Try: Cut your contract review from 4 hours to 12 minutes.

Not: AI-powered customer support.
Try: Resolve 80% of support tickets before a human sees them.

Not: AI-powered data analysis.
Try: Turn 6 weeks of manual reporting into one-click dashboards.

See the difference?

The first version tells them what you are.
The second tells them what changes in their day.

Your buyer doesn't care about your technology.
They care about getting home before 7pm.
They care about not hiring three more analysts.
They care about closing their quarter without panic.

Specificity is the antidote to AI buzzword fatigue.

When you name the exact problem and the exact outcome,
you're not competing with every AI company anymore.
You're the only one talking about their actual Tuesday afternoon.

That's how you stand out.
That's how you get the demo.
That's how you close faster.

Think your messaging is specific enough?
Roast your Ads at AdRoast.in

To address concerns of alleged bias, fraud, or misconduct in Alberta's private business sector—particularly within contracts, corporate research, and compensation—a structured, evidence-based approach is necessary. 

Below is a framework grounded in legal, economic, and forensic accounting principles to ensure redress and accountability:

 **1. Legal and Contractual Recourse** - **Contract Auditing**: Systematic review of contractual terms using legal doctrines (such as undue influence and unconscionability) and computational text analysis to detect irregularities. 
- **Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR)**: Mediation or arbitration under the Alberta Arbitration Act to resolve disputes efficiently before litigation. 
- **Whistleblower Protections**: Use of Alberta's Public Interest Disclosure Act (if applicable) or federal protections under the Canada Labour Code for corporate misconduct reporting.
 **2. Forensic and Economic Investigation**
 - **Benford's Law Application**: Statistical anomaly detection in financial records to identify potential fraud (such as manipulated compensation figures). 

- **Regression Analysis**: Econometric modeling to assess wage disparities or biased compensation structures against industry benchmarks.
 - **Digital Forensics**: Metadata scrutiny in electronic contracts and research documentation to trace alterations or fraudulent backdating. **3. Research Integrity Measures** 
- **Peer Review Scrutiny**: Independent replication studies to verify corporate-sponsored research validity. 
- **Conflict of Interest Disclosures**: Mandatory transparency in funding sources under Canadian Anti-Corruption Legislation.
 - **Bias Audits**: Algorithmic fairness assessments if AI-driven decisions influence contractual or hiring outcomes. 
**4. Stakeholder Advocacy and Policy Reform** 
- **Class Action Litigation**: Where systemic misconduct is identified, collective legal action under Alberta's Class Proceedings Act. 

- **Regulatory Engagement**: Formal complaints to the Alberta Securities Commission (ASC) or Competition Bureau Canada for market manipulation or fraud. 

- **Legislative Proposals**: Proposals for amendments to Alberta's Business Corporations Act enhancing auditor independence and penalties for misconduct. 

**Key Considerations** - **Burden of Proof**: Stakeholders must provide prima facie evidence (documented discrepancies, statistical outliers) before formal proceedings. 

- **Jurisdictional Limits**: Cross-border issues may invoke the UNCITRAL Model Law or bilateral treaties for enforcement. This framework balances empiricism with legal pragmatism, ensuring stakeholders pursue redress through defensible, methodologically sound channels.

What if the job offer you just negotiated was never really a negotiation?

The salary. The terms. The "market data" behind it.

All of it shaped—before you walked in—by an AI system that already knew how likely you were to accept, how far you'd push back, and the minimum required to close.

This is already happening. In hiring. In insurance claims. In supplier contracts.

And in most cases, the person on the other side has no idea.

The most important question isn't operational.

It's ethical: would you be comfortable disclosing to the other party that they're negotiating with a machine?

If the answer is no, that tells you everything.

I've been thinking about this a lot, and wrote more about it here:

That number alone should be a wake-up call for every organisation across the GCC.

Cyber threats are no longer limited to ransomware or large-scale breaches. Organisations are now dealing with constant phishing attempts, AI-driven attacks, deepfakes, insider threats, malware, identity theft, and attacks targeting critical infrastructure.

The biggest risk is no longer whether your business will be targeted.

It is whether you have the right people in place to detect threats early, respond quickly, and protect your environment before serious damage is done.

Across the UAE and KSA, we are seeing a huge increase in demand for:
• SOC Analysts
• DFIR Specialists
• Threat Hunters
• Incident Response Teams
• SIEM Engineers
• OT Security Specialists
• IAM Professionals
• GRC Specialists
• Cybersecurity Leaders

At Cander, we support organisations across the GCC with both permanent recruitment and project-based contract hiring across cybersecurity and technology.

Whether you need to build a SOC function, strengthen your cyber defence team, support a major transformation programme, or deploy contractors quickly into your environment, we can help.

Cybersecurity is no longer just an IT issue. It is a business-critical priority.
If you are looking to strengthen your cyber capability across the UAE or KSA, feel free to get in touch.

Artificial Intelligence is reshaping the world of work by redefining job roles and driving efficiencies in workplace management, while also introducing new challenges in employment law.
How should we approach this significant transformation?
 
From 22 to 24 April, our Partners Alessandra Ferroni, Saverio Schiavone, Cristina Capitanio and Emanuele Panattoni will be attending the 𝐼𝐵𝐴 𝐸𝑚𝑝𝑙𝑜𝑦𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐷𝑖𝑣𝑒𝑟𝑠𝑖𝑡𝑦 𝐿𝑎𝑤 𝐶𝑜𝑛𝑓𝑒𝑟𝑒𝑛𝑐𝑒 in Warsaw, organised by the International Bar Association to explore the evolving intersection of AI and employment law.
 
This three-day event will focus on topics such as algorithmic hiring, workplace surveillance, data privacy, bias and discrimination in AI systems, and the implications of automation for labour rights and employment contracts.
The conference will also examine regulatory developments across jurisdictions and provide employers practical guidance on navigating compliance in this rapidly changing landscape.
 
👉 If you are attending the event, please feel free to get in touch with our experts!

The Content Marketing Institute’s 2026 Career & Salary Outlook dropped at the perfect time. 

I’m wrapping up a contract role, so I find myself on the hunt for a new full-time gig. And two insights from the report give me HOPE:

➡️ AI is causing more work, not less: We thought AI was going to take our jobs away, and that’s not the case. 76% of marketers feel they care doing the work of more than one job and 59% say their workloads are overwhelming. Sounds like I could help with that…
➡️The surprise expansion of hiring overall, concentrated at more senior levels: CMI says this is good for Gen X and bad for Gen Z – lucky for me, I’m smack dab in the middle as a millennial. Fingers crossed this tracks for my job search… 

What’s keeping me grounded: CMI’s research shows job searches are taking longer, which makes sense with an inundated market due to an increase in layoffs (if you can imagine it could be any worse than just two years ago). 

And that’s where you come in, my friends.

In this age of AI and all this fancy-schmancy technology, I believe the best way to be seen is to have an “in.” If you know of a content marketing opportunity at your company or see a role pop up in your feed, please keep me in mind! My DMs are open and I’m ready to do the damn thing. But I’m keenly aware that I can’t do it on my own. Any help is most appreciated. 

These are just my takeaways from the CMI 2026 Career & Salary Outlook, so grab your copy and share what you think!! 

Content Marketing Institute #contentmarketing #jobsearch

Your AI transformation isn’t slowing down because of AI.

It’s slowing down because your org is still built for 2015.

⸻

Recent McKinsey findings are blunt:

Top-performing companies don’t treat tech as execution.

They put tech leadership inside strategy.

⸻

Most financial institutions still don’t.

CIOs are handed roadmaps they didn’t shape
then expected to “deliver transformation.”

That gap is the problem.

⸻

Three shifts are separating leaders from laggards:

⸻

1. The junior-heavy dev model is breaking

Agentic systems are absorbing a large share of repetitive build work.

agentic AI

Hiring for volume now adds cost without proportional output.

Demand is shifting toward:

→ senior engineers
→ system architects
→ people who orchestrate humans + agents

⸻

2. Seat-based vendor contracts are outdated

Paying for “people on a project” no longer maps to value.

One simple shift is emerging:

→ contracts tied to productivity outcomes

Legal tech funding in Q1 2026 sent a pretty clear message.
Investors are doubling down on AI‑native platforms with a very specific job 
And they aren't generic “legal AI” toys.





If you ignore the mega late‑stage and debt deals, the biggest checks went to five companies that all share the same pattern. 

> Tight ICP
> Deep workflow
> Clear ROI.

The real top 5 (pre‑Series D, no debt)

Fieldguide | $75M | Growth
  - GRC platform that automates audits, controls, and compliance work (SOC 2, ISO, public‑company audits).  
  - Who they serve: Audit, risk, and compliance leaders inside large enterprises.

Factify | $73M | Seed
  - AI‑powered document intelligence for due diligence, contract review, and evidence analysis.  
  - Who they serve: Law firms and in‑house teams buried in documents for M&A and litigation.

Lawhive | $60M | Series B
  - AI‑native “next‑gen law firm” and legal services platform.  
  - Who they serve: Consumers and small businesses that have historically been priced out of traditional firms.

Crosby | $60M | Series B
  - AI‑powered contract review “neofirm” where AI agents and lawyers review contracts by the page.  
  - Who they serve: Fast‑growing tech companies and in‑house legal teams with high contract volume.

Orbital (Orbital Witness) | $60M | Series B  
  - AI for witness, e‑discovery, and deposition support. Transcribing, tagging, and analyzing testimony and documents.  
  - Who they serve: Litigation and investigations teams dealing with complex, sensitive matters.

Right behind them, you see Ivo (~$55M) and Summize (~$50M), which only reinforces the same story: focused AI + workflow depth wins.
________________________
A few takeaways Black Gavel is paying attention to:

> These products are specialists, not generalists. Each lives inside a specific, painful workflow: audits, DD, consumer matters, contracts, or disputes. 

Not general “legal work."

> The ICP is crystal clear. Every one of these companies can point to a specific buyer and a specific P&L line they impact. 

Billable hours, review time, compliance cost, or risk.
__________________________
Who’s writing the checks?

> Heavyweights like Sequoia Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, GV (Google Ventures), Accel, Index Ventures, Lux Capital, and Balderton are actively backing legal‑AI from Seed through Series C.

> The LegalTech Fund raised a $110M second fund focused solely on this space, plus thematic investors such as Second Century Ventures and Anthos Capital.

If you’re building, investing, or hiring in legal tech, Q1 is a good reminder:  

> Pick a lane
> Own the workflow
> Prove ROI

Position: Principal AI Data Architect 
Location: US\Canada - Remote
AI-Ready Data Platform · ML/LLMOps · Agentic AI Infrastructure · Governance & Security 
Location: Canada |  Department: Data & AI Engineering   
 
ABOUT THE ROLE 
We are hiring a Principal Data Architect — a hands-on, senior individual contributor who will design, 
build, govern, and evolve the single source of truth that powers every AI initiative in our organisation. 
This platform will serve as the foundational nervous system for conversational AI assistants, dashboard 
intelligence, autonomous AI agents, RAG-powered applications, predictive ML models, and any AI 
product we build today or in the future. 
You will architect the system, drive implementation, own the data contracts that agents and AI 
applications depend on, enforce security and access governance for both human and agent consumers, 
and continuously monitor and improve the accuracy and reliability of AI outputs that flow from this 
platform. You are the person who ensures our AI systems are only as good as the data beneath them — 
and you make that data exceptional. 

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR 
Must-Have Experience 
• 15+ years of hands-on data engineering and architecture experience, with 3–5+ years building 
production AI/ML and LLM-era data infrastructure. 
• Proven experience designing enterprise-scale AI data platforms that serve multiple AI consumers — 
not just one application or pipeline. 
• Deep expertise in lakehouse and data mesh architectures: Databricks, Delta Lake, PySpark, Kafka, 
Spark Structured Streaming, cloud-native data services (AWS, Azure). 
• Hands-on experience with vector stores, semantic models, knowledge graphs, and retrieval 
infrastructure in production environments. 
• Working knowledge of LLMOps: model serving pipelines, MLflow, CI/CD for AI, automated 
evaluation, and production monitoring. 
• Strong background in data governance, security, and compliance in regulated industries (financial 
services, payments, cybersecurity, healthcare). 
• Experience defining data access controls for AI agents and automated systems — not just human 
users. 
Technical Skills 
• Expert: Python, SQL, PySpark, Kafka, Databricks, Delta Lake, Snowflake,AWS (S3, Glue, EKS, Bedrock, 
Kinesis, Redshift), Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, GitHub Actions. 
• Strong: LangChain, LlamaIdex, LLM APIs (OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Claude, HuggingFace), vector 
databases (Pinecone, FAISS, ChromaDB, OpenSearch), knowledge graphs (Neo4j). 
• Solid: MLflow, FastAPI, CI/CD pipelines, observability tooling (CloudWatch, Grafana, or equivalent), 
data lineage and metadata management platforms.

🚀 Hiring: SAP ISU Billing Functional Consultant
📍 Location: 100% Remote
💼 Experience: 10+ yrs

We are seeking an expert SAP-ISU Billing Functional Consultant for the BRIGHT program, a large-scale EDM billing transformation for one of the largest utility companies in the U.S. This role is for the final, most technically demanding phase—focusing on complex multi-Op Co validation.

🔹 Core Technical Requirements:

SAP-ISU Billing & EDM: Hands-on configuration of billing schemas, rate categories, and the Real-Time Pricing (RTP) interface.

Complex Rate Logic: Deep expertise in demand charges, Time-of-Use (TOU) rates, ratchet clauses, and multi-tier logic.

End-to-End M2C: Comprehensive knowledge of the Meter-to-Cash process, including interval meter data and exception management.
Specific Billing Riders: Experience with SMS riders, interruptible service, and purchase power riders.

Testing & Validation: Lead/Support for FUT, SIT, and regression testing for high-complexity billing functionality.

🔹 Key Responsibilities:
Configuration: Validate complex billing contracts and offline bill print customization across multiple jurisdictions (GS, TX, LA, MS).
Solutioning: Lead design sessions for specialized contracts (Economic development, special rate contracts).

Agile Collaboration: Translate business requirements from Product Owners into technical configurations within an active sprint environment.
AI Requirement Review: Review and gap-fill AI-generated requirements to ensure all edge cases are captured before development.

Documentation: Maintain the program knowledge base for configuration decisions and design rationale.

✨ Preferred Qualifications:
Prior experience with Entergy systems or multi-jurisdictional utility profiles.
Experience with S/4HANA Utilities migration or Cloud EDM environments.
Familiarity with AI-assisted requirement generation workflows.

*Experience with Einstein Studio, Data Cloud retrievers, and RAG. Expertise in data modeling, data warehousing concepts, and data governance frameworks.

Headline: We’re Hiring! Experienced BD Professional (Remote)
Are you a master of the freelance marketplace? Do you know how to turn a cold profile into a lead-generating machine?
System Logicx is expanding, and we are looking for a high-energy, experienced Business Development Executive to lead our growth on global platforms like Upwork, Fiverr, and Freelancer.
This is a remote, commission-based role designed for a closer who wants to be rewarded directly for their impact. If you have the strategy to build agency profiles and the grit to win high-ticket technical projects, we want you on our team.
The Role at a Glance
Profile Building: Optimize and manage our presence on Upwork, Fiverr, and Freelancer.
Lead Generation: Source and bid on technical projects that align with our expertise.
Proposal Strategy: Write high-converting, personalized proposals.
Remote Work: Full flexibility to work from anywhere.
Earnings: Highly competitive, performance-based commission structure.
What We’re Looking For
Proven Track Record: You’ve successfully managed agency or individual profiles with a history of winning contracts.
Tech-Savvy: You understand the software development landscape (Web, Mobile, AI).
Communication Pro: Exceptional English writing and negotiation skills.
Self-Starter: You don’t need a micromanager; you need a technical team that can deliver on the promises you make to clients.
Ready to Scale With Us?
Join a team that provides the technical muscle while you provide the strategic growth.
Apply Now:
Send your profile/portfolio or interest to:
📧 systemlogicx@gmail.com

Hi All,

Greetings of the day

We are Hiring for AI/ML Solution Architect - Remote

Please check the below job details and share your resume at: naveen.reddy@theavianconsulting.com

Role: AI/ML Solution Architect
Location: Remote
Duration: Contract

Note: only Citizens

We are seeking an experienced AI/ML Solution Architect with strong expertise in Microsoft Copilot, Azure services, Python, and PySpark. The ideal candidate will play a key role in designing, architecting, and delivering AI/ML-driven solutions for enterprise environments. This role requires deep technical understanding, hands-on development skills, and the ability to collaborate with cross-functional business and engineering teams.

Key Responsibilities:
Lead end-to-end architecture and solution design for AI/ML projects in a cloud environment.
Develop scalable data and ML pipelines using Python, PySpark, and Azure Databricks.
Integrate, configure, and optimize Microsoft Copilot capabilities within business applications.
Collaborate with data scientists, engineers, and product teams to build robust AI solutions.
Drive best practices for model development, versioning, deployment, and monitoring.
Support cloud modernization initiatives and architecture reviews.
Clearly document architectural designs, patterns, and implementation details.

Required Skills & Experience:

8+ years of experience in software engineering / data engineering / solution architecture roles.
Strong experience building and deploying ML solutions on Azure (Azure ML, Azure Data Factory, Storage, AKS, etc.).
Hands-on expertise in Python and PySpark for data processing and model development.
Experience with Copilot / AI copilots / Generative AI integration and solutioning.
Strong understanding of cloud-based architecture, CI/CD, DevOps, data governance, and security.
Ability to lead technical discussions, solution design sessions, and architecture reviews.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

Good-to-Have Skills:
Experience with LLMs, OpenAI APIs, or custom model fine-tuning.
Knowledge of Databricks, delta lakes, and large-scale distributed systems.
Microsoft Azure certifications (Architect, Data Engineer, AI Engineer).

🚨 Hiring: SDET – Python + AI / Test Automation (Agent-Based Testing) 🚨
📍 Location: Remote
🧑‍💼 Experience: 10+ Years
💼 Employment Type: Contract 
We are looking for a highly skilled SDET with Python and AI-driven testing expertise to build next-generation intelligent test automation solutions. If you have experience in LLM validation, agent-based testing, and automation frameworks, this is a great opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI systems.
🔹 Key Responsibilities:
• Design and develop scalable automation frameworks using Python (Pytest / Playwright / Requests)
• Build AI-powered test agents for autonomous testing and validation
• Validate LLM-based applications (semantic similarity, hallucination detection, response validation)
• Implement prompt-based testing & adversarial scenarios
• Develop API, UI, and microservices test automation
• Integrate automation into CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions / Jenkins / Azure DevOps)
• Create test data strategies including synthetic data generation
• Implement observability-driven validation (logs, metrics, traces)
• Ensure high reliability and low flakiness in distributed environments
🔹 Required Skills:
✔ Strong programming in Python
✔ Hands-on with Pytest, Playwright, Selenium
✔ Strong experience in API testing (Requests / HTTP clients)
✔ Experience designing scalable automation frameworks
✔ Strong understanding of microservices & event-driven systems (Kafka / queues)
🔹 DevOps & Cloud:
✔ Experience with CI/CD tools (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, Azure DevOps)
✔ Hands-on with Docker / containerization
✔ Familiarity with AWS / Azure
📩 Interested candidates can share their resume at:
Deepakn@arborteksys.com
#SDET #Python #AI #TestAutomation #LLM #GenerativeAI #Hiring #RemoteJobs #AutomationTesting #Playwright #Pytest #Selenium #DevOps #Cloud #Kafka #TechJobs #HiringNow #ImmediateHiring 🚀

extracted.full_content:
Hi All,

Greetings of the day

We are Hiring for AI/ML Solution Architect - Remote

Please check the below job details and share your resume at: naveen.reddy@theavianconsulting.com

Role: AI/ML Solution Architect
Location: Remote
Duration: Contract

Note: only Citizens

We are seeking an experienced AI/ML Solution Architect with strong expertise in Microsoft Copilot, Azure services, Python, and PySpark. The ideal candidate will play a key role in designing, architecting, and delivering AI/ML-driven solutions for enterprise environments. This role requires deep technical understanding, hands-on development skills, and the ability to collaborate with cross-functional business and engineering teams.

Key Responsibilities:
Lead end-to-end architecture and solution design for AI/ML projects in a cloud environment.
Develop scalable data and ML pipelines using Python, PySpark, and Azure Databricks.
Integrate, configure, and optimize Microsoft Copilot capabilities within business applications.
Collaborate with data scientists, engineers, and product teams to build robust AI solutions.
Drive best practices for model development, versioning, deployment, and monitoring.
Support cloud modernization initiatives and architecture reviews.
Clearly document architectural designs, patterns, and implementation details.

Required Skills & Experience:

8+ years of experience in software engineering / data engineering / solution architecture roles.
Strong experience building and deploying ML solutions on Azure (Azure ML, Azure Data Factory, Storage, AKS, etc.).
Hands-on expertise in Python and PySpark for data processing and model development.
Experience with Copilot / AI copilots / Generative AI integration and solutioning.
Strong understanding of cloud-based architecture, CI/CD, DevOps, data governance, and security.
Ability to lead technical discussions, solution design sessions, and architecture reviews.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

Good-to-Have Skills:
Experience with LLMs, OpenAI APIs, or custom model fine-tuning.
Knowledge of Databricks, delta lakes, and large-scale distributed systems.
Microsoft Azure certifications (Architect, Data Engineer, AI Engineer).
